chunk_map.heading: H1: The check you built is an eval (lede: "Look at what you just shipped.")
One primitive, placed wherever there's a bar
The primitive that runs on cadence
Why the loop survives the model

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 0 line 3 "Look at what you just shipped" and chunk 1 line 7 "The thing you built reads an agent's work" — session-artifact carve-out (lecture placed directly after the M6 exercise, per Lecture meta: "closing lecture for AE101 M6... Names evals with full weight from the skill you just authored"). chunk 2 line 14 "The shape fires on any workflow" and chunk 4 line 25 header "The primitive that runs on cadence" — "the shape"/"the primitive" resolve under a sequential read to "eval", named in chunk 1 and reused across the deck (arc-earned within-file term, not a cold referent under the v2 sequential test).
chunk 4 line 27's reference-page link is a navigational pointer (carve-out), not a positional callback.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 1 line 7 "the failure shape you saw at M5"; chunk 3 line 20 "your verifier from M5, scaled" — both cross-module recaps expressly kept under the maintainer-attested `check_lectures §3` consolidation carve-out (this lecture's M2/M5 refs), not dead beats.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 5 line 32 checked for anaphora triple ("Reference because…Plan because…Verifier because…") — each leg carries distinct concrete content (forgets / window fills / plausible-but-wrong), not filler repetition; not flagged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
